stories.title: How Pedro Lourtie became the EU’s dealmaker in chief
stories.summary: How Pedro Lourtie became the EU’s dealmaker in chief Council President António Costa’s chief of staff is in charge of building consensus between capitals. It’s a job that’s only getting harder. By GABRIEL GAVIN in Brussels Illustration by Natália Delgado/ POLITICO When the motorcades carrying the EU’s presidents and prime ministers pull into Brussels for high-stakes negotiations every few months, it’s Pedro Lourtie’s job to make sure they leave with a deal. In

stories.title: Ünneplés közben megsérült az angol válogatott egyik játékosa a vb-n, hordágyon vitték az öltözőbe
stories.summary: Jordan Henderson a reklámtáblát akarta átugorni, de beakadt a lába, és csúnya esés lett belőle.

stories.title: „Már a reptérre menet sejtettük, hogy gond lesz, mert nagyon füstölt a vulkán” – magyarokkal is kibabrált a pöfékelő Etna
stories.summary: Hétfő estig lezárták a cataniai repteret, emiatt a Budapestről induló járatokat több órás várakozás után Palermóba irányították. Azok a magyar utasok sem jártak jobban, akik Cataniából repültek volna haza.

stories.title: Orosz rakéták és drónok rázták meg Kijevet, több civil meghalt a hajnali támadásban
stories.summary: Számos épület megrongálódott, a romokból több embert ki kellett menteni.

stories.title: Májusban elkezdett magára találni a magyar ipar
stories.summary: Sok ipari alágban még mindig csökkent a termelés éves alapon, a járműgyártás és az elektronikai ipari viszont bővülni tudott.

stories.title: Elromlott az MR-gép a szolnoki kórházban, ahol Magyar Péter korábban beszorult a liftbe
stories.summary: Az egészségügy nyilatkozatstopjának megszüntetése után tett közzé közleményt az ország egyik legrosszabb állapotú kórháza.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint az óbudai korrupciós ügy megmutatja kicsiben, hogyan osztotta fel az országot a Fidesz és az MSZP
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök nagykoalíciós ügynek nevezte a botrányt.

stories.title: Kátyús úton a Porsche is csak vánszorog
stories.summary: Magyarországon sok alapvető szektorban mindent felülírt az alkalmazkodás a politikai feltételekhez, és ennek hatása a teljes gazdaságra átterjedt. Jelentős haszonnal kecsegtet, ha sikerül ezen a helyzeten javítani.

stories.title: Új euró államkötvényeket bocsát ki Magyarország
stories.summary: A devizakötvényekre a felszabadult uniós forrásokból tervezett projektek előfinanszírozásához lehet szükség.

stories.title: Az első tiszás parlament Magyar Péter nélkül, Ruff és Vitézy nagy napja – országgyűlés és elemzés
stories.summary: Nagyívű Alaptörvény-módosítást nyújtott be a miniszterelnök, majd elutazott a NATO-csúcsra és nyaralni. A Fidesz már alkotmányos válságot emleget, és több kulcsemberüket is támadja a Tisza. Vitézy Dávid nyitja a parlamentet, eközben csúcson a Tisza támogatottsága. Élő közvetítés és elemzés Cseke Balázzsal, a Telex belpolitikai újságírójával.

stories.title: Ruff Bálint elnézést kért egy fideszestől
stories.summary: Vitézy felszólalásával indult a parlamenti ülés, a Fidesz öt hazugságáról beszélt. Tuzson Bence Hitlert emlegette, a KDNP-s Juhász Hajnalka bocsánatot kért a gyermekvédelem áldozataitól, majd megvédte Semjén Zsoltot. Magyar Péter a NATO-csúcsra utazott, nélküle tartanak ülést a képviselők.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor szerint a Tisza minden határt átlép: emberit, erkölcsit, jogállamit
stories.summary: A Fidesz elnöke tüntetésre hív a Sándor-palotához, de nem tudni, felszólal-e ott.

stories.title: Brazília kamatostul megfizette az árát, hogy két kulcsposzton elfogytak
stories.summary: Középpályán a 34 éves Casemirónak kellett volna bírnia a világbajnokság tempóját, Norvégia ellen azonban olyan kezdett középen, aki évek óta nem játszott ezen a poszton.

stories.title: A kormányváltás az akkugyártók kommunikációjában is fordulatot hozott
stories.summary: Nem volt még egy ágazat Magyarországon, amely annyira elzárkózott a nyilvánosságtól, mint az akkuipar. Most már a gödi Samsung SDI is nyitni igyekszik.

stories.title: Európa szorult helyzetből várja a NATO-csúcsot és Trumpot
stories.summary: Az egész világ Ankarára figyel, ahol a tagállamoknak részletesen ismertetniük kell, hogyan érik el a védelmi kiadások növelését. Putyin előtte hosszan beszélt Trumppal, aki Zelenszkijjel viszont személyesen is találkozik. Kérdés, hogy Trump páros lábbal száll-e bele szövetségeseibe az iráni háborús sérelmei miatt. Magyar Péter először találkozik majd az amerikai elnökkel.

stories.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
stories.summary: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

stories.title: Turkey intensifies crackdown on public life in run-up to Nato summit in Ankara
stories.summary: More than 200 arrested in raids, comedian and journalists jailed, gay-friendly cruise turned away and protests banned Authorities in Turkey have widened a crackdown on public life, arresting more than 200 people during raids across Ankara last month, jailing a comedian and blocking a cruise ship carrying LGBTQ+ passengers from docking in the run-up to the Nato summit in the capital. The arrests followed a ban on demonstrations in Ankara that was put in place until 10 July. Human Rights Watch (HR

stories.title: Nigeria says two nationals killed in South Africa amid rise of anti-migrant attacks
stories.summary: Nigeria's government said one of its nationals was reportedly killed by police officers "using gruesome interrogation techniques".

stories.title: Britain heads to NATO summit with no funding plan
stories.summary: ABOARD THE HMS PRINCE OF WALES — The U.K. will show up at this week’s NATO summit with no long-term plan to reach alliance spending targets, something Defence Secretary Dan Jarvis told POLITICO is “mission critical” to deliver as soon as possible. “Those were the assurances that I was giving to NATO partners when I was in Brussels a couple of weeks ago. That’s what I will be saying to people in Ankara,” he said aboard the HMS Prince of Wales aircraft carrier sailing near the Arctic Circle. Donal

stories.title: Britain intercepted Russian aircraft in expanded NATO Arctic mission
stories.summary: ABOARD THE HMS PRINCE OF WALES — Britain intercepted a Russian plane after moving an aircraft carrier to the Arctic Circle to support NATO missions. The Ministry of Defence said Monday that a Russian “Bear F” maritime patrol aircraft had repeatedly approached the U.K.’s carrier strike group in the Norwegian sea in an “unsafe and unprofessional” maneuver late last week. It said the Russian aircraft had dropped a large number of sonobuoys — devices that can act as underwater microphones to find an

stories.title: Zelenskyy demands Western support after Russian strikes kill 12 in Kyiv
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy renewed his calls for more Patriot missiles Monday as Russia’s latest missile attacks killed at least 12 people and injured more than 50 in Kyiv on Sunday night. “It is critically important that the world — first and foremost the United States and our European partners — come out of the NATO summit in Turkey with strong decisions in support of our air defense,” Zelenskyy wrote on X on Monday. Zelenskyy had warned late Sunday that intellig

stories.title: A Place in the Sun review – subversive exposé of picture-postcard luxury in the Canary Islands
stories.summary: Documentary intersperses pastel scenes of spotless tourist resorts with candid interviews with the asylum seekers who labour to keep them pristine Every year, millions of tourists flock to the Canary Islands, a Spanish archipelago in the Atlantic Ocean where the sun always shines. Through static shots resembling picture postcards, Mette Carla Albrechtsen’s pastel-hued documentary captures the leisure activities on offer: cerulean rooftop pools, pristine sandy beaches and raves that stretch into

stories.title: One Nation to contest WA byelection triggered by Labor MP’s resignation – as it happened

stories.title: Zelenskyy calls for ‘strong decisions’ at Nato summit as Russia kills 21 in overnight strikes on Kyiv – Europe live
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president makes plea ahead of summit this week in Ankara where he is due to meet Trump on the sidelines Meanwhile, the UK has just sanctioned Russian actors involved in researching, developing and producing the novichok nerve agent and the lethal toxic Epibatidine, used in the Salisbury attack and the poisoning of Alexei Navalny. Among those sanctions are SC Signal, a Russian state scientific research institute, as well as three individuals researching novichok and Epibatidine. “Russia

stories.title: Celtic leaders doubt Burnham’s devolution drive will go beyond England’s borders
stories.summary: Would-be prime minister has made basic missteps in pitches to Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland Andy Burnham’s devolution promises are yet to impress sceptical Celtic administrations hoping for a reset with Westminster, sources in Cardiff and Edinburgh have said. Burnham, who is expected to take over from Keir Starmer as prime minister on 20 July, has made much of his support for the devolution of power and resources in England, pledging in an agenda-setting speech last week to make a new “No

stories.title: Germany news: Federal Cabinet approves draft 2027 budget
stories.summary: The budget draft provides for more borrowing than initially planned, as well as a big increase in defense spending. Meanwhile, three men are facing trial over a massive stash of illegal weapons. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 22 killed in Kyiv region
stories.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

stories.title: EU Commission considers supercharged department for foreign relations
stories.summary: The European Commission is considering combining several units into one powerful department for external relations, three EU officials told POLITICO’s Brussels Playbook . The idea — one of several options being considered to boost the Commission’s external-facing policy units — would be to maximize the EU executive’s external-policy reach, in keeping with the aims of a “ large-scale review ” being led by Budget Commissioner Piotr Serafin. A supercharged departmen

stories.title: Inside the Telegram ‘drug rape’ chat group that shocked Germany
stories.summary: The men, who drugged and secretly filmed their victims, were members of an online gang targeting mainly women of Chinese heritage Early last year, Ivy*, a Chinese student living in Germany, got a phone call from the police. At first, she thought it was a scam. An officer said they needed her help with an investigation involving someone she once knew. What followed turned her world upside down. The police told her that her former boyfriend, identified in court documents as Tong Z, had been invest

stories.title: Millions join funeral procession for Iran’s supreme leader Ali Khamenei
stories.summary: Crowds swelled through Tehran as mourners dressed in black carried flags proclaiming: ‘We will rise’ A crowd of millions assembled on Monday for the funeral procession of Iran’s assassinated supreme leader, Ali Khamenei . The scale and depth of the march, however engineered, represents an extraordinary turnaround for a country that only seven months ago was gripped by street protests at which thousands of people were killed by government security forces. Many will say the assembly was a monument

stories.title: China tests long-range missile in South Pacific in move Australia condemns as ‘destabilising to region’
stories.summary: Launch comes just hours after Australia and Fiji sign defence agreement as expert says timing not a coincidence China has conducted a long-range missile test in the South Pacific just hours after Australia signed a defence agreement with Fiji, sparking condemnation from Canberra and regional leaders. The Australian foreign minister, Penny Wong, said the missile test was “destabilising” to the region, while her New Zealand counterpart, Winston Peters, described it as “deeply concerning”. Continue

stories.title: Tennessee national guard troops shoot man dead in Memphis
stories.summary: Authorities claim Tyrin Johnson, 20, pulled gun on officers controversially deployed at instigation of Trump National guard soldiers on patrol in Memphis shot and killed a man that authorities in the Tennessee city said turned and pulled a gun on the troops during a chase. The shooting took place at about 4am on Sunday as the soldiers responded to a report of gunfire. Continue reading...

stories.title: Russian strikes on Kyiv expose widening air defence gaps on eve of Nato summit
stories.summary: Second assault on capital and surrounding region within days leaves 19 dead and buildings heavily damaged Europe live – latest updates A wave of Russian missiles and drones has struck Kyiv, killing 19 people and heavily damaging apartment blocks and other buildings, in an attack on the eve of a Nato summit in Turkey that has exposed widening gaps in Ukraine’s air defences. Thirteen people were killed in the capital, which was Russia’s main target, and 56 were injured, according to the city’s adm

stories.title: Trump denied latest bid to delay $5.8m judgment payment to E Jean Carroll
stories.summary: Carroll was awarded damages after New York jury concluded Trump sexually abused her, then defamed her after she publicly described the attack Donald Trump ’s latest attempt to delay payment of a $5.8m judgment for defaming a magazine columnist whom a jury determined he sexually abused has been emphatically rejected by a federal court judge. In a single-sentence 4 July order, US district Judge Lewis Kaplan denied the president’s request for more time to pay the civil judgment owed to E Jean Carro

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Funeral procession for supreme leader Ali Khamenei begins in Iran
stories.summary: Millions join funeral procession in capital of Tehran to mourn Khamenei who was kiled in US-Israel airstrikes in February Lebanese state media said an Israeli strike on a car in the country’s south on Monday killed four people, including three women, despite a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. stories.summary: Reform UK leader claims he is victim of ‘hit job’ as parliamentary standards commissioner investigates £5m donation On the subject of Andy Burnham, the Financial Times is running a story today saying the access talks his team is holding with senior civil servants, intended to help Whitehall departments get ready to implement the new PM’s policy agenda, are being hindered by the fact that Burnham has not decided who will do the top cabinet jobs. stories.title: Higher-earning Australians flocking to 5% first home deposit scheme with some borrowers earning over $200,000
stories.summary: Exclusive: Economists warn Labor’s removal of caps could be pushing up prices as support flows to people in better financial positions Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Most Australian first home buyers are using the government’s 5% deposit scheme, with one in three new participants earning more than the scheme’s previous cap for high income earners. The influx of high income earners into the first home guarantee program, economists warn, has pushed up property prices

stories.title: Florida Republican says deporting Haitians with TPS would be ‘huge mistake’
stories.summary: ‘Haiti is a failed state’, says Carlos Giménez, congressman and Miami Cuban exile, after controversial court ruling Carlos Giménez, a Republican congressman from Florida, broke with the Trump administration on Sunday, calling on the White House to reconsider its push to eliminate temporary protected status (TPS) for Haitian migrants. Returning some 350,000 Haitians to their chaotic, dangerous homeland following the US supreme court’s ruling that the Trump administration can cut off temporary leg

stories.title: Philippine Senate launches impeachment trial of Sara Duterte
stories.summary: The trial against Sara Duterte is the latest escalation in a bitter feud between the Philippines' leading political dynasties. The proceedings could have major implications for the country's 2028 presidential elections.

stories.title: Why many immigrants leave Germany again
stories.summary: Germany been attracting many skilled workers to its labor market. The challenge, however, is that many of them do not stay. So what is going wrong?

stories.title: Can India, Europe build a clean energy supply chain beyond China?
stories.summary: With China dominating every step of the solar energy production chain, governments in India and Europe are looking to reduce their overreliance on a single supplier.

stories.title: Why Ukrainian strikes on annexed Crimea are a blow to Putin
stories.summary: Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Crimea is a target for Kyiv, and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin.

stories.title: Australia and Fiji seal mutual defence pact in push to counter China in Pacific
stories.summary: The signing of the defence treaty comes on the same day that Chinese state media reported a submarine had test-launched a long-range ballistic missile in the South Pacific, a move criticised by Australia.

stories.title: At least 19 killed and more than 100 wounded in Sri Lanka prison riot, officials say
stories.summary: The wounded were rushed to the Negombo hospital, north of the capital Colombo, following overnight clashes between inmates from two drug gangs, police said.

stories.title: Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review Balogun ban
stories.summary: President Donald Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review United States striker Folarin Balogun's one-match suspension at the World Cup.

stories.title: The EU that the UK left no longer exists | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Andy Burnham’s rise has stoked talk of the terms for a future British return – but this is the wrong question The question of the UK’s relationship with the EU has resurfaced with Keir Starmer’s premiership drawing to a close and Andy Burnham, his likely successor, preparing to enter No 10. Wes Streeting, until recently one of the contenders for the top job and now a possible future chancellor of the exchequer, went as far as to say recently that Britain should be back in the EU . stories.title: Turkey blocks cruise ship carrying 2,000 LGBTQ+ passengers and a ‘furious’ Patti LuPone, citing ‘moral values’
stories.summary: Broadway star, who is performing on the vessel, expresses shock after authorities ban Scarlet Lady from docking in Kuşadası A cruise ship carrying 2,000 LGBTQ+ passengers and the Broadway performer Patti LuPone has been blocked from entering Turkey after local authorities said their behaviour didn’t “align with the structure of our society and our moral values”. Virgin Voyages’ Scarlet Lady set sail from Athens, Greece, on 5 July for what was billed as “an epic all-gay voyage” over 10 days run b

stories.title: Israeli command system identified 850,000 targets in Gaza and Lebanon wars, says supplier
stories.summary: Elbit Systems supplied Tzayad digital army programme to map people, vehicles and other objects in real time Israel identified about 1,000 potential targets a day during the first two years of the wars in Gaza and Lebanon with its command and control system, according to a presentation by the country’s largest arms supplier, Elbit Systems. A total of 850,000 targets were detected in real time by the Israeli Tzayad digital army programme across all the military’s theatres of war between 7 October

stories.title: ‘Better safe than sorry’: Greece installs floating barrier to ward off toxic fish
stories.summary: Climate crisis and warming waters have attracted long-toothed pufferfish to new parts of the Mediterranean From his deckchair, his arms thrown above his head, his feet sliding back and forth in the sand, Pavlos Beleyiannis watches his grandchildren bathe in his favourite bay. It’s an idyllic scene, infused with a serenity that the newly retired truck driver attributes squarely to a sense of security. For the first time, a floating barrier has been installed across the bay. Ducking, splashing and

stories.title: Weather tracker: Europe braces for another heat surge as tropical nights return
stories.summary: Spain, Portugal, France and UK face spell of high temperatures, while Super Typhoon Bavi barrels through north-western Pacific Europe live – latest updates Another surge of heat spread across western Europe at the weekend, with Spain, Portugal and France already sweltering and southern parts of the UK joining them on Monday. Temperatures are once again forecast to climb to 10-15C above average, with highs approaching 40C (104F) in the hottest parts of France and Spain, while the UK is expected t

stories.title: Wildfires rage across southern Europe, forcing thousands to flee homes
stories.summary: Tour de France spectator ban as country along with Spain, Portugal and Greece faces ‘powder keg’ after heatwave Europe live – latest updates Wildfires raging across southern Europe have forced thousands to flee their homes and prompted officials to ban spectators from a stage of the Tour de France, amid warnings of “powder keg” conditions after a record-breaking early summer heatwave. Hundreds of firefighters are tackling blazes that have burned through almost 20,000 hectares (49,500 acres) in P

stories.title: The Guest review – Trine Dyrholm pulls out all the stops as a bipolar mother in dysfunctional family drama
stories.summary: Karlovy Vary film festival Writer-director Mads Mengel’s film about a seaside christening disrupted by a previously shunned relative is shot in the spirit of Dogme 95 Danish actor Trine Dyrholm gives a magnetic performance with all guns blazing in this intensely painful, uncomfortable but also sometimes uncomfortably funny film from writer-director Mads Mengel; it is about a dysfunctional family and is shot in a freewheeling handheld style with lots of looming extreme closeups, a film in the spi

stories.title: ‘Smart operator’: how BT’s first female CEO helped turn company around
stories.summary: The firm’s share price has risen 80% under Allison Kirkby’s leadership – but pressure remains for her to deliver further growth Business live – latest updates If timing is everything, then Allison Kirkby may have judged it perfectly. Since becoming BT’s first female chief executive more than two years ago the company’s share price has climbed 80%, an investor-pleasing turnaround that has seen Kirkby well-rewarded with a pay and bonus package of £5.6m last year , the largest for a boss of the tel

stories.title: Wegovy weight-loss pill goes on sale on UK high street and online pharmacies
stories.summary: Thousands of people begin receiving their first deliveries of once-a-day medication made by Novo Nordisk Business live – latest updates A once-a-day Wegovy weight-loss pill has gone on sale at high street and online pharmacies in the UK, but is not yet available on the NHS. Thousands of people began receiving their first deliveries of the pill, made by the Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk, on Monday. It contains the same active GLP-1 ingredient as the Wegovy jab, semaglutide, and is similarly effec

stories.title: Iran begins long farewell to former supreme leader with calls for Trump’s death | First Thing
stories.summary: Millions gather in streets and officials appear in public at Tehran funeral of Ali Khamenei in show of defiance. Plus, origin of mysterious ‘space balls’ in Australia revealed Don’t already get First Thing in your inbox? Sign up here Good morning. Iran’s week of mass funeral processions ⁠for the former supreme leader Ali Khamenei has seen public calls for the killing of Donald Trump . stories.title: Detained Gaza doctor almost unrecognisable after injuries in Israeli jail, lawyer says
stories.summary: Hussam Abu Safiya faces ‘tangible danger to his life’ following 18 months in prison without charge or trial Middle East crisis live – latest updates One of Gaza’s most prominent doctors is almost unrecognisable because of severe injuries inflicted in Israeli detention, his lawyer has said, and faces “tangible danger to his life” after being held for 18 months without charge or trial. Hussam Abu Safiya met his lawyer on 2 July, after a transfer to Israel’s notorious underground Rakefet prison in

stories.title: GB News co-owner ‘cashing in on climate chaos’ after leap in fossil fuel investments, critics say
stories.summary: Exclusive: Campaigners argue news channel’s attacks on climate action ‘work in financial interests’ of Sir Paul Marshall The hedge fund run by the co-owner of GB News almost tripled its investments in fossil fuel companies in the first quarter of 2026 to $2.8bn (£2.1bn), the Guardian can reveal. stories.title: UK charity funding school at heart of illegal Israeli settlement expansion
stories.summary: Friends of Yeshivat Shavei Hevron sent nearly £200,000 to school in Hebron – where Israel has been accused of imposing apartheid – between 2019 and 2024 A British charity is funding a religious school at the heart of expansion plans for the illegal Israeli settlement in the Palestinian city of Hebron. stories.title: Trump rings New York Stock Exchange bell to mark first trading day for ‘Trump accounts’
stories.summary: US president rings bell from White House and showcases initiative that gives children a $1,000 investment account Donald Trump rang the bell of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) from the White House to mark the first trading day for Trump accounts, a government initiative that provides children with a $1,000 investment account. The US president hosted leaders from the NYSE and the Nasdaq stock exchange at the Oval Office for a press conference on Monday morning. It is the first joint opening of

stories.title: Extreme high temperatures double young people’s risk of mental health admissions, Australian research shows
stories.summary: Exclusive University of Sydney study finds heat-related hospital admissions to increase by 6% to 7.7% by the end of the century Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Extreme high temperatures in warmer months double the risk of young people being admitted to hospital for a mental health condition, according to research. The analysis, which looked at 720,000 New South Wales hospital admissions of young people aged up to 24, suggested the climate crisis would aggravate the a

stories.title: European space telescope Euclid spots oldest quasars yet
stories.summary: Think of quasars as time machines. Rare but revealing. They are light from the beginnings of the universe that let astronomers investigate how early galaxies formed.

stories.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
stories.summary: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

stories.title: Why heat waves heighten the risk of blackouts
stories.summary: When extreme heat hits, people turn to AC, but what if the grid fails? From France to the US, heat waves are pushing power systems to the limit, raising blackout risks and energy prices when electricity is needed most.

stories.title: China tests long-range missile in South Pacific
stories.summary: Beijing carried out the launch in what is known as a nuclear-free zone due to a longstanding treaty against testing nuclear missiles in the area.

stories.title: Iran: Millions flock to Ali Khamenei's funeral procession
stories.summary: Crowds have thronged the streets of Tehran in a procession for Iran's late supreme leader. The huge event is seen as a show of strength by the theocratic regime amid peace negotiations with the US.

stories.title: Hamas to dissolve Gaza governing body
stories.summary: Officials for the militant organization have confirmed the move, which was agreed to earlier this year in a peace deal with Israel.

stories.title: Sri Lanka prison clashes leave dozens dead
stories.summary: More than two dozen people have died and over 100 injured following a violent dispute between different prison populations in Negombo.

stories.title: Firefighters battle wildfires across southern Europe
stories.summary: Wildfires are raging across several parts of southern Europe. They come on the heels of massive heat waves in much of the region.

stories.title: Is Africa ready for El Nino?
stories.summary: Experts warn the El Nino climate phenomenon could bring drought, floods and displacement to parts of Africa later this year. Are governments prepared?

stories.title: Weapons of long-ago conflicts still haunt the people of Laos
stories.summary: As decommissioning teams seek out unexploded ordnance in paddy fields, forests and rural pastures, Laos prepares to host a review conference of the Convention on Cluster Munitions.

stories.title: 'Major damage' as Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands
stories.summary: The eye of the Category 5 super typhoon passed over the tiny island of Rota Monday morning local time, bringing winds of more than 241 km/h, also hitting the nearby islands of Guam, Tinian and Saipan.

stories.title: Thousands of forgotten WW1 soldiers recognised for first time
stories.summary: Nearly 10,000 soldiers' names are being added to the official database of war dead, a cherished step for many of their descendants.

stories.title: 'I ate ketchup and cheese,' says Venezuelan girl trapped under quake rubble for 32 hours
stories.summary: Fabiana was trapped in the rubble of a 10-storey residential building after two earthquakes rocked Venezuela in June.

stories.title: Activists covered in red paint protest bullfighting in Pamplona
stories.summary: Animal rights activists covered in red paint protest bullfighting in Pamplona before the San Fermin Festival, calling for an end to the tradition.

stories.title: Ali Khamenei's coffin carried through the streets of Tehran
stories.summary: Thousands of mourners gathered in Tehran as Iran began funeral ceremonies for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: Girl trapped in Venezuela quake 'survived on ketchup and cheese'
stories.summary: BBC international correspondent Yogita Limayee meets 12-year-old Fabiana, who was trapped in Venezuela's devastating earthquakes.

stories.title: Mexico fans left heartbroken as World Cup dream comes to an end
stories.summary: Mexico fans in Mexico City react with joy and heartbreak as England win 3-2 in a World Cup last-16 match, ending Mexico’s tournament run.

stories.title: Why an anti-sacrilege law in India's Punjab has sparked controversy
stories.summary: The dispute has revived one of Punjab's most sensitive political and religious issues ahead of state elections next year.

stories.title: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
stories.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.

stories.title: Joy for England fans after dramatic World Cup victory
stories.summary: England fans celebrated their team's dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico as the Three Lions booked a place in the World Cup quarter-finals.

stories.title: Hamas announces dissolution of Gaza governing body, paves way for technocratic committee
stories.summary: The move marks a significant political shift by Hamas, which has run Gaza since its fighters seized control from rival Palestinian movement Fatah in 2007.

stories.title: Wildfire rages in eastern Spain, forcing hundreds to flee
stories.summary: A wildfire in Castellon, eastern Spain, forces 500 evacuations as firefighters and aircraft battle flames spreading across the Valencia region.

stories.title: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
stories.summary: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

stories.title: Watch: Russian attack leaves gaping hole in Kyiv residential building
stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

stories.title: Wildfire in southern France forces evacuation of 10,000 people
stories.summary: Tour de France organisers ban spectators from stage three as a wildfire hits the Pyrénées-Orientales region.

stories.title: Sri Lanka prison riots leave 26 dead and more than 100 injured
stories.summary: The two days of violence at Negombo Prison are the worst prison riots in the country in years.

stories.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why this moment matters for France
stories.summary: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.

stories.title: Bangladesh courts China even as ties with India improve
stories.summary: Relations between Dhaka and Delhi, that turned frosty under the interim government, are on the mend.

stories.title: Rodríguez defends Venezuela’s emergency earthquake response as number of bodies expected to soar
stories.summary: Interim president says unrest will not break out despite anger at official response to the 24 June disaster Venezuela’s interim president has defended her country’s emergency response to the twin earthquakes that have killed more than 3,000 people, vowing the country would not descend into social unrest. Many Venezuelans have expressed anger at what they see as the US-backed government’s inadequate response to the 24 June disaster before international teams arrived. Continue reading...

stories.title: Wildfires rage in Portugal, Greece, France and Spain
stories.summary: Six countries in southern Europe have been hit by wildfires. One of the blazes, in southwestern France, could affect Monday's third stage of the Tour de France, authorities warn.

stories.title: Far-right AfD reaffirms ambition to govern Germany
stories.summary: The AfD has strengthened its hardline leadership despite ongoing scrutiny from Germany's domestic intelligence agencies. Co-leader Alice Weidel says the far-right party is ready to govern after strong polling gains.

stories.title: Super Typhoon Bavi hits US Pacific islands
stories.summary: Super Typhoon Bavi made landfall near Guam, a US territory located in the western Pacific Ocean. The storm brought powerful winds and heavy rain to the Northern Mariana Islands.

stories.title: Ukraine: Several killed in renewed attacks on Kyiv
stories.summary: At least 12 people were killed when Russian ballistic missiles hit the Ukrainian capital. This follows the large-scale attack on Kyiv last Thursday.

stories.title: Australia, Fiji sign defense pact to counter China in Pacific
stories.summary: Fiji, which had grown closer to Beijing under its former leadership, will now consult Canberra on security developments.

stories.title: Emotion and politics merge in Tehran at funeral of former supreme leader
stories.summary: The BBC's chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet is in Tehran, where funeral events are taking place in honour of Iran's former leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: 'Definitely felt a big bang': Delta plane hit by firework while landing in Chicago
stories.summary: The plane had 52 passengers and six crew onboard. No injuries were reported.

stories.title: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

stories.title: The African fishermen who blame Chinese trawlers for their woes
stories.summary: Fishing crews in Sierra Leone say large Chinese ships are illegally hoovering up stocks.

stories.title: Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands with huge wind gusts
stories.summary: The storm, with winds of nearly 290km/h (180mph) and gusts of 350km/h, is lashing the island of Rota.

stories.title: From 'a beautiful night' to 'world's greatest hangover', guests react to Taylor and Travis's wedding
stories.summary: Some of the famous guests have been posting on social media about the big day in New York.

stories.title: Australian PM apologises for 'inappropriate' comments about Kylie Minogue
stories.summary: Albanese has apologised 'unequivocally' after his remarks prompted a backlash.

stories.title: Australia space agency has found 'likely source' of mystery space balls
stories.summary: The agency says that they "appear to be pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle".

stories.title: Zivatarokra és erős szélre számíthatunk a hét első felében, de alapvetően meleg lesz az idő
stories.summary: Hétfőre elsőfokú figyelmeztetést adtak ki zivatarok miatt, kedden már 33 fok is lehet.

stories.title: Olyan Fidesz–MSZP közös lopást vallott be Óbuda volt alpolgármestere, amiről az elmúlt 30 évben csak legendák szóltak
stories.summary: Czeglédy Gergő, Óbuda volt MSZP-s alpolgármestere részletes beismerő vallomásában arról beszélt, hogyan szervezték a kerületi korrupciót Puskás Péterrel, a Fidesz helyi erős emberével. A tökélyre fejlesztett, kétszintes kenőpénzes rendszer állítása szerint a kerület fideszes és baloldali vezetése alatt is működni tudott. Puskást kerestük ezekkel az állításokkal kapcsolatban, de kérdéseinkre nem reagált.

stories.title: Luxus-bevásárlóutcát is kapott volna a Tőzsdepalotába megálmodott Nemzeti Galéria
stories.summary: Piero Lissoni olasz építész kiposztolta, hogyan alakította volna át a Tiborcz István érdekeltségébe tartozó épületet.

stories.title: Bocsánatot kért az ausztrál miniszterelnök, amiért azt mondta, lefeküdne Kylie Minogue-gal
stories.summary: Antony Albanese egy podcastben azt mondta, le is feküdne az énekesnővel.

stories.title: Ezt nehéz nevetés nélkül kibírni: Harry Kane-nek interjú közben ment el a hangja
stories.summary: „Szóhoz sem jutok. A szavam is elakad” – mondta elcsukló hangon az angol válogatott csapatkapitánya, és igaza volt.

stories.title: Az erdőtüzek miatt nézők nélkül rendezik meg a Tour de France 3. szakaszának befutóját
stories.summary: Több mint 700 tűzoltó és 9 helikopter próbálja megfékezni a Franciaország déli részén pusztító tüzeket.

stories.title: Személyesen tárgyal az orosz–ukrán háború lezárásáról Trump és Zelenszkij a törökországi NATO-csúcson
stories.summary: Az eseményen több milliárd dolláros beruházásokat is bejelenthetnek.

stories.title: A befektetők csaknem 4 milliárd dollárt buktak, Trump 600 milliót keresett saját kriptovalutáján
stories.summary: Trump lelkesen biztatta híveit, hogy fektessenek be, de ők nem jártak nagyon jól. Az elnök viszont akkor is rengeteget keres a kriptovalutáján, ha az árfolyam lefelé tart.

stories.title: Vitézy az új éjszakai járaton beszélt arról, hogy buszsávot terveznek a Budakeszi útra
stories.summary: Félkész tervek már vannak, de a kivitelezés átmeneti nehézségekkel járhat.

stories.title: Vége a szuperruhakorszaknak, megdöntötte a legrégebbi úszó világcsúcsot a kanadai csodaúszó
stories.summary: Summer McIntosh az utolsó olyan egyéni csúcsot döntötte meg, amit még cápadresszben úsztak.

stories.title: Pörgött a kiskereskedelem a választások utáni hónapban
stories.summary: Májusban éves és havi alapon is nőtt a kiskereskedelmi forgalom, főleg a nem élelmiszert áruló boltokban vásároltak többet az emberek a KSH adatai szerint.

stories.title: Donald Trump lobbizhatta ki a FIFA-elnöknél, hogy függesszék fel az amerikai csatár eltiltását a foci-vb-n
stories.summary: A háromgólos Folarin Balogun piros lapot kapott Bosznia-Hercegovina ellen, Belgium ellen mégis játszhat.

stories.title: Kedden eldől, hogy indulhat-e a 2027-es elnökválasztáson Marine Le Pen
stories.summary: Döntést hoz a fellebbviteli bíróság, miután a Nemzeti Tömörülés politikusát tavaly öt évre eltiltották közfeladat viselésétől.

stories.title: Új mérföldkő az űrkutatásban: felfedezte a világegyetem két legtávolabbi ismert kvazárját a Euclid űrtávcső
stories.summary: A legtávolabbi objektum fénye akkor indult útnak, amikor a világegyetem mindössze 670 millió éves volt.

stories.title: Kapitány István kirúgta az Eximbank vezetőjét
stories.summary: Berta Adriennt, aki egy éve vezeti az Eximet, minden más vezető beosztásából is visszahívják.

stories.title: Harry herceget meghívták a Buckingham-palotába, de nem válaszolt időben, ezért mégsem mehet
stories.summary: Rövid időre úgy tűnt, békülőben lehet a királyi családdal hat éve szakító herceg és a brit uralkodó, aztán a palota rendet tett.

stories.title: Az egygyermekes családmodell a leggyakoribb Európában
stories.summary: Hollandia kivételével tavaly minden uniós tagállamban több volt a gyermekes háztartásokon belül az, amelyben egy gyermek élt, mint amelyben kettő vagy több.

stories.title: Tipikus nyári események, amikor különösen fontos a tudatos hidratálás (x)
stories.summary: Fesztiválok, bringatúrák, váratlan nyári záporok. Az évszak tele van olyan élménydús eseményekkel, amelyek gyakran kimozdítanak a komfortzónánkból és felborítják a megszokott rutinunkat. Ezekben a helyzetekben a tudatos hidratálás kifejezetten fontos, ha nemcsak a kellemes íz és a frissítő élmény számít, hanem az immunrendszer normál működésének támogatása is.

stories.title: A Kormányzati Ellenőrzési Hivatal kérésére adta ki Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz egészségügyi adatait a Honvédkórház
stories.summary: Az viszont kérdés, hogy ki férhetett hozzá ezekhez az adatokhoz.

stories.title: Összeszólalkozott a kapussal, majd zokogva a földre rogyott – így ért véget Neymar pályafutása a brazil válogatottban
stories.summary: Brazília elmúlt évtizedének legjobb játékosa a negyedik vb-jén is betalált, de sokra nem ment vele.

stories.title: Az UEFA szerint a FIFA átlépett egy vörös vonalat Folarin Balogun eltiltásának felfüggesztésével
stories.summary: A volt FIFA-elnök Sepp Blatter is keményen beszólt Gianni Infantinónak. Szerinte a futball nem lehet Trump és a politika játékszere.

stories.title: Zelenszkij szerint Ukrajnának nincs elég légvédelmi rakétája az orosz ballisztikus rakéták leszedéséhez
stories.summary: 19 ember meghalt a Kijevet ért orosz támadássorozatban az éjjel, az orosz ballisztikus rakétákat nem tudták kivédeni.

stories.title: Már a tragikus baleset napján is szabálytalankodott: a győri Aston Martin-os gázoló a bíróságon azt állította, nem tudja, miért adott gázt
stories.summary: A február 14-i halálos gázolás elkövetője nemrég egy elektromos rollerrel is balesetet szenvedett: összetörte az arcát, állkapcsa leszakadt, és három műtéten esett át.

stories.title: Magzatokat pakoltak a képviselők asztalaira Dúró Dóráék, a parlamenti dolgozók rögtön leszedték őket
stories.summary: A műanyag magzatokat az ülés kezdete előtt osztották ki a Mi Hazánk politikusai, de nem sokáig maradtak az asztalokon.

stories.title: Csütörtökön tüntet a Fidesz az alkotmánymódosítás ellen a Sándor-palotánál
stories.summary: „Minden eshetőség felmerült” – mondta Gulyás Gergely arról, hogy felmerült-e a Fidesz–KDNP-ben a parlamenti munka bojkottálása.

stories.title: Gajdos László minisztériuma felülvizsgálja az ebrendészeti telepek működését
stories.summary: Gajdos azt mondta, szívügye, hogy ezek a telepek „megfelelő színvonalúak legyenek, és nagyon-nagyon tartsák be az állatjóléti szempontokat”.

stories.title: Vizsgálatot rendelt el Forsthoffer Ágnes, amiért Bohár Dániel a Fidesz vendégeként dolgozik a Parlamentben
stories.summary: Az egyik házelnöki rendelkezés értelmében sajtótudósítói tevékenységet csak sajtóbelépővel rendelkező újságírók végezhetnek az Országgyűlés épületeiben. Bohárnak ilyenje nincs.

stories.title: Hivatalos: július végén megszűnik az MCC alapítványa
stories.summary: Ruff Bálint határozott erről.

stories.title: Kilencből csak egy ember kért bocsánatot Semjén Zsolttól azok közül, akiket a „Zsolti bácsi”-ügyben feljelentett
stories.summary: A rágalmazás miatt feljelentett Szabó Tímea, Arató Gergely és Somogyi András humorista éppen a bizonyítási eljárás lefolytatását szeretnék elérni, és akár poligráfos vizsgálatnak is alávetnék Semjént.

stories.title: Környezetkárosítás miatt nyomoz a rendőrség a debreceni Semcorp ügyében
stories.summary: A feltárt szabálytalanságok miatt a kormányhivatal pár napja minden tevékenységet megtiltott a gyárban.

stories.title: Húszmilliós osztalékot hozott tavaly a Puskás-hagyaték, ennek közel felét Szöllősi György kapja
stories.summary: Majdnem ötször annyit, mint Puskás Ferenc unokája.

stories.title: „Szerintem nem volt szabálytalanság” – Trump elismerte, ő kérte a FIFA-elnököt az amerikai eltiltás visszavonására
stories.summary: De csak kért, nem mondta meg Gianni Infantinónak, mit kell csinálnia.

stories.title: Az MCC vezetősége szerint „semmilyen váratlan vagy rendkívüli fejlemény nem történt”
stories.summary: Konstruktívan együttműködnek a kormánnyal, és szeretnék, ha az eredeti alapító más szervezeti formában, zökkenőmentesen működtethetné tovább az intézményt.

stories.title: Ukrán dróntámadás érte Oroszország legnagyobb olajfinomítóját
stories.summary: A háború kezdete óta ez a legnagyobb hatótávolságú dróntámadás: az omszki olajfinomító 2700 kilométerre fekszik az ukránok által ellenőrzött területektől.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
stories.summary: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

stories.title: Albania PM Rama: Protests a stage for anti-Trump forces
stories.summary: Protesters in Albania say their government is selling the country its nature out to foreign investors and luxury developers. Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama tells DW why he thinks people are so upset.

stories.title: Celtic nations begin to plan for breakup of UK in event of Reform election win
stories.summary: Politicians brace for constitutional turmoil if Nigel Farage’s party ends up in government – or even as a strong opposition The rise of Nigel Farage has prompted political leaders across Ireland, Northern Ireland, Scotland and Wales to game the unthinkable: the breakup of the United Kingdom. Unionists who wish to save the union and nationalists who wish to end it are bracing for constitutional turmoil if Reform UK emerges triumphant – with Farage as prime minister or official leader of the oppos

stories.title: Archaeologists uncover ancient Byzantine city in Egypt’s western desert
stories.summary: Well-preserved fourth-century quarters reveal details of daily life, urban development and economic activities Archaeologists in Egypt have uncovered a well-preserved Byzantine-era city in the western desert. The fourth-century quarters had residential and religious structures, including a basilica-style church in the Dakhla oasis. Archaeologists also found coins, pottery fragments and tools. Continue reading...

stories.title: Founder of prominent underground church released from prison in China
stories.summary: Ezra Jin, founder of Zion Church, lands in the US and reunites with family who are ‘overwhelmed with joy’ The founder of one of China’s most prominent underground churches has been released from prison and reunited with his family in the United States. Ezra Jin, the founder of Zion Church, landed in the US on Friday evening. He was one of dozens of church members who were detained in a sweeping crackdown on Christians in October . Continue reading...

stories.title: Bellingham két perc alatt duplázott, az angolok tíz emberrel nyerték meg a vb eddigi legjobb meccsét
stories.summary: Őrült fordulatokat hozott a Mexikó–Anglia nyolcaddöntő az Azteca stadionban. Harry Kane-ék megszenvedtek a 3-2-es győzelemért.

stories.title: Jehovah's Witnesses fight German state for Holocaust archive
stories.summary: The Jehovah's Witnesses have secured a partial victory in a dispute over a unique archive documenting the community's persecution by the Nazi regime. It comes after a new memorial to the victims was unveiled in Berlin.

stories.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
stories.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

stories.title: Fireworks light up Washington sky for America's 250th anniversary
stories.summary: A fireworks display illuminated the National Mall in Washington as the United States marked the 250th anniversary of its independence.

stories.title: 'The American Dream is back,' Trump says during Fourth of July speech
stories.summary: "Our warriors did not fight communism on battlefields across the world only to have that menace rear its ugly head right back here in America," Trump told a cheering crowd.

stories.title: Zelenskyy and Putin hold Fourth of July phone calls with Trump
stories.summary: Yuri Ushakov, a Russian presidential aide, said Putin and Trump discussed "the issue of the Ukrainian settlement" in a phone call that lasted an hour and twenty-five minutes.

stories.title: Eulogist at Khamenei funeral in Iran calls for death of Donald Trump
stories.summary: Trump said in January that he had given “very firm instructions” to “wipe them off the face of this Earth” if Iran ever tried to assassinate him.

stories.title: Cargo ship reports being attacked in the Red Sea, maritime agency says
stories.summary: "Vessels are advised to transit with caution and report any suspicious activity to UKMTO," the agency said.

stories.title: JD Vance says 'beautiful' Britain has been 'failed by its leadership'
stories.summary: While he called Britain "amazing," Vance said there appeared to him to be something "very broken about British politics".

stories.title: New pipeline in Canada to proceed after C$150bn pledged to ease BC and First Nations concerns
stories.summary: Port expansion and protections for whales part of BC and Alberta plan to expand country’s presence overseas The governments of Canada and the province of Alberta will move forward on a major new oil pipeline after the pair announced a plan to ease concerns of British Columbia and First Nations on the Pacific coast. Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, shuttled between British Columbia and Alberta on Thursday to announce more than C$150bn in new investments in both provinces, part of a broader p

stories.title: Overseas education project for women and girls axed by UK after two years
stories.summary: The programme, aimed at keeping 1m girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, withdrawn after aid cuts A leading higher education programme, aimed at keeping 1 million girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, has been axed by the British government just two years after it was announced. The scheme, Strengthening higher education for female empowerment (SHEFE), which was unveiled with some fanfare two years ago by the outgoing Conservative government, had a £45m budg

stories.title: UK woman, 21, arrested in Thailand after allegedly stabbing boyfriend to death
stories.summary: Officers found body of 34-year-old man in luxury rental home in Pattaya area, local media says A 21-year-old British woman has been arrested in Thailand after allegedly fatally stabbing her boyfriend, according to local media reports. The Bangkok Post reported that on Thursday morning local time, officers found the body of a 34-year-old man, who operated a cannabis farm, in a luxury rental home in the Pattaya area, a beachside region two hours from Bangkok known for its large expat population an

stories.title: ‘The situation is terrible’: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes
stories.summary: El Obeid becomes key battleground in war between Sudan’s armed forces and their paramilitary enemies, the RSF Fatima has lost count of the number of drone attacks on the besieged city of El Obeid in Sudan, but said the attacks this past weekend were the most violent so far. The drones hit schools and fuel stations, killing more than 20 people, including students, she said. “Over the past few months, seeing 40 or 45 drones is the norm. You can literally count them,” said the aid volunteer, whose

stories.title: Welcoming the Taliban? — Germany's new Afghanistan policy
stories.summary: To facilitate the deportation of criminals to Afghanistan, the German government is allowing Taliban diplomats into the country.

stories.title: Iran FM confirms reports Israel planned to target Tehran's negotiators
stories.summary: Iran's foreign minister corroborated US media reports that Washington warned Tehran Israel may have had its chief negotiators on a target list during April talks in Islamabad, the same talks that produced the framework deal signed on 17 June.

stories.title: Did he say 'Bismillah'? Cristiano Ronaldo’s penalty whisper ignites global debate
stories.summary: Superstar striker Cristiano Ronaldo’s whisper broadcast live around the world before the decisive penalty against Croatia sparked a global debate over whether it was an Arabic-inspired sacred invocation or his own pre-Saudi encouragement routine in Portuguese.

stories.title: Conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori wins Peru's presidential election in runoff
stories.summary: Voters were primarily concerned with increasing levels of crime, especially extortion by violent organized crime gangs, and Fujimori pledged to combat crime with an iron fist.

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Russian airstrikes on Kyiv, the aftermath of the earthquakes in Venezuela, a brutal heatwave in Europe and Harry Kane at the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Pope Leo praises US history of welcoming immigrants at 250th anniversary – video
stories.summary: Pope Leo has used his first major address to his home country to praise the US's history of ⁠welcoming immigrants, urging Americans to live up to the ideals put forward in the ⁠declaration of independence. In a speech broadcast live from the Vatican to the National Constitution Center in Philadelphia ‌as he received the Center’s liberty medal, Leo said he hoped that ideals ​of 'unity, justice and peace' held by the founding fathers would guide the US as it celebrated its 250th anniversary Pope p

stories.title: How the 1986 Mexico World Cup was almost cancelled after a devastating earthquake
stories.summary: Guardian reports after the disaster told of 5,000 deaths, much of the capital being razed, and doubts about Mexico hosting the finals Mexico last hosted the World Cup in 1986 , but the competition was almost cancelled several months before the start when an earthquake struck the capital, Mexico City, leaving at least 5,000 people dead, 30,000 homeless and much of the city flattened, in one of the worst earthquakes to hit the country. To this day, the death toll remains disputed, with some estima

stories.title: Maltese tycoon faces trial over Caruana Galizia murder
stories.summary: The Maltese businessman accused of masterminding journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 murder is in court — seven years after his arrest. Five other men have already been found guilty for their roles in the murder.

stories.title: Mourners in Damascus bury cafe bomb blast victims as officials promise justice
stories.summary: Although there is so far no indication who carried out the attack, many were quick to blame loyalists of former President Bashar al-Assad, who was ousted in an insurgent offensive in December 2024.

stories.title: EBRD backs Kazakhstan's move up the minerals value chain with €255m loan
stories.summary: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) is financing Kazakhstan's first large-scale refractory gold ore processing facility, helping the country unlock previously inaccessible reserves while expanding domestic mineral processing.

stories.title: 'Vanishingly rare' copy of US Declaration of Independence discovered in the UK
stories.summary: Graham Moore, the Revolution 250 curator at the National Archives, said the "exceptional" item was "one of the rarest forms of the Declaration we know about".

stories.title: Cape Verde fans stand proud despite Argentina defeat
stories.summary: Cape Verde supporters expressed pride in their team's historic World Cup campaign despite a 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina.

stories.title: Iranians mourn as Khamenei funeral ceremonies begin
stories.summary: Large crowds gathered in Tehran as Iran began six days of funeral ceremonies for late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed during the US-Israeli conflict.

stories.title: Trump warns of communist threat as Washington parade cancelled due to heatwave
stories.summary: "Communism is a mortal threat to American liberty," Trump said during a speech on Friday. "It is the greatest threat to our country".

stories.title: World leaders and royals celebrate the US's 250th birthday
stories.summary: "The story of the relationship between our two nations is one of extraordinary evolution, from overcoming conflict to forging one of the closest and most productive alliances the world has seen," King Charles III said.

stories.title: Russia's fuel crisis: Is Putin under pressure?
stories.summary: Ukraine's extended campaign against Russian energy infrastructure is disrupting fuel supplies and military logistics. With shortages mounting in occupied Crimea, is the pressure pushing Putin toward negotiations?

stories.title: Turkish comedian arrested for 'insulting' Islam and Erdogan
stories.summary: Turkish comedian Deniz Göktaş was arrested at Istanbul Airport on Thursday following a stand-up show posted to YouTube. The 32-year-old faces multiple probes, including "publicly insulting religious values" and insulting President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

stories.title: ‘Humanity is a privilege’: Umar Khalid on his six years in an Indian jail without trial
stories.summary: Exclusive: Activist tells of his life as one of India’s most prominent political prisoners and his opposition to the government of Narendra Modi Prison is hardest at sunset. As the thousands of prisoners incarcerated in Delhi’s most infamous jail are cast out of their cells and forced into the dank yard until darkness falls, prisoner number 626714 feels the punishing dread begin to rise. Yet the inmate – better known as Umar Khalid – was recently moved to discover that another political prisoner

stories.title: Delhi plans to ban petrol rickshaws and scooters in effort to cut toxic fumes
stories.summary: Government hopes for 30% of city’s fleet to be electric by 2030, in move hailed as ‘gamechanger’ on air pollution The unruly chaos of Delhi’s roads would be unrecognisable without the rickshaws and scooters that zip through India’s capital in their millions, emitting toxic fumes in their wake. But now, ambitious policies aim to give the city’s most recognisable vehicles an environmental makeover. On Monday, Delhi’s government announced plans to eventually ban petrol scooters, motorbikes and auto

stories.title: Pakistan roof collapse kills 14 children at tutoring centre
stories.summary: Local officials said preliminary reports showed the centre was unregistered and operating inside a privately owned residential building Fourteen children died after ⁠the roof of a tutoring centre collapsed in Pakistan’s eastern city of ⁠Lahore on Tuesday, ⁠rescue ​officials have said, as authorities opened the way for a possible negligence ⁠investigation. Punjab’s emergency service said rescuers found children and a 30-year-old female teacher ⁠under the rubble of the private after-school facilit

stories.title: Côte d’Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
stories.summary: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and Nigeria Floods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan. There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘I can only describe it as a war zone’: the rescuers navigating Venezuela’s post-quake hellscape
stories.summary: Thousands of volunteers are joined by overseas teams in the hope of finding more survivors in the rubble, reports Tom Phillips in Caraballeda. Photography and video by Manu Quintero When twin earthquakes tore through Venezuela’s northern coast last week, Israel Rivas was at home hundreds of miles away in the industrial city of San Félix. As the scale of the catastrophe became clear, the 24-year-old knew he had to react. A mechanic and budding photographer, Rivas gathered the money he had been sa

stories.title: Wildfires rage in parts of France and Spain after record heatwave – video
stories.summary: Numerous wildfires are raging across parts of France and Spain, after record temperatures in Europe caused dry conditions and a worsening drought. Two thousand firefighters battled several wildfires fanned by strong winds along France's Mediterranean coast on Thursday, while Spain’s military emergency unit (UME) said it had deployed more than 100 personnel and about 40 vehicles to support firefighting efforts in the Aragon region Weather tracker: Heatwave breaks June temperature records across E

stories.title: Explosion in Monaco: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev?
stories.summary: Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was reportedly injured in a suspected assassination attempt in Monaco. Kyiv had previously imposed sanctions on him. What is known about the case?

stories.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
stories.summary: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

stories.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
stories.summary: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

stories.title: Air conditioner rush: Scuffles break out at Lidl stores across France
stories.summary: Hundreds of shoppers queued outside Lidl supermarkets across France on Thursday after the retailer announced the sale of 200,000 fans and portable air conditioners, triggering long lines, scuffles and police intervention at several stores.

stories.title: Venezuela's interim leader defends earthquake response as death toll tops 2,500
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez has hit back at criticism over her government’s response to the quakes, rejecting accusations of slow rescue efforts and claims that official casualty figures have been under-reported.

stories.title: EU halves duty-free steel quota but UK and other partners given better rate
stories.summary: Thirteen countries with a free trade agreement with Brussels have their quota reduced by just one-third Business live – latest updates The EU has halved the amount of duty-free steel it will accept from abroad, but has agreed higher import volumes for more than a dozen trading partners, including Britain. However, some steel producers have been hit harder than others with Tata Steel UK, Britain’s biggest producer, revealing its duty-free exports have been slashed by 60%. Continue reading...

stories.title: EU urged to exempt UK from car rules that could be worst Brexit impact yet
stories.summary: Bloc’s ‘made in Europe’ regulations risk shutting out British manufacturers in ‘most spectacular own goal in history’ The EU’s car industry has called for the UK to be fully included in new “made in Europe” rules that threaten to shut out British manufacturers from their biggest export market. The European Automobile Manufacturers Association (Acea) on Wednesday urged Brussels to give the UK, Turkey and Morocco “justified, targeted exemptions” to the rules, which will require cars and parts to b

stories.title: At least nine monks killed in Thailand after boy drives truck into procession
stories.summary: Charges yet to be filed over incident in Mukdahan involving 11-year-old, as police seek to establish circumstances An 11-year-old boy has driven his parents’ truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing at least nine monks. CCTV footage shared by a local rescue group showed the moment the monks, wearing orange robes, were run over as they walked in procession along a road. The timestamp on the footage was shortly before 11am local time on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
stories.summary: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipient As Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister. Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. Continue reading...

stories.title: China says man who flew plane into Beijing skyscraper had mental health problems
stories.summary: Official statement offers the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual and fatal incident in Beijing Chinese authorities said the man who flew a small plane into Beijing’s tallest skyscraper last week was a 66-year-old who had mental health problems. The statement published on Thursday offered the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual incident that occurred in Beijing’s central business district on the evening of 26 June. Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave breaks June temperature records across Europe
stories.summary: UK provisional peak of 37.7C shatters previous record by huge margin, while Germany hits all-time high of 41.7C Last week’s heatwave across western Europe shattered national June records and set new all-time highs. The UK recorded a provisional high of 37.7C at Lingwood in Norfolk on Friday 27 June, smashing the previous June record of 35.6C, set in 1976. Such a margin is exceptionally rare: temperature records are typically broken by 0.1C or 0.2C, not a remarkable 2.1C. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hydration breaks: The most controversial issue at the World Cup
stories.summary: The mandatory hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup have sparked debate in the stands, on the pitch, and far beyond.

stories.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
stories.summary: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has repercussions.

stories.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada
stories.summary: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

stories.title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
stories.summary: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

stories.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
stories.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

stories.title: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers
stories.summary: The Vatican's watchdog authority has imposed severe disciplinary measures on an ultratraditionalist Catholic group. The move comes after the group, the Society of Saint Pius X, consecrated bishops without papal consent.

stories.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
stories.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

stories.title: German public university creates Islamic theology faculty
stories.summary: The University of Münster is the first public university in Europe to establish an Islamic theology faculty, a move that is is attracting international attention.

stories.title: German coalition agrees on changes to pensions, tax rates
stories.summary: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government plans to push through sweeping changes with the stated goal of reviving a sluggish economy. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

stories.title: AI drones made in Germany see duty on Ukraine's front line
stories.summary: The German defense company Helsing is supplying combat drones to Ukraine and will soon provide them to the German army. How effective are they on the front line? DW joined a Ukrainian combat mission to find out.

stories.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
stories.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?
